## Value bullets (for any talking-point moment)

- You've earned a strong word-of-mouth reputation in Stockbridge, but right now it lives on scattered directory pages — Yahoo, Nextdoor, BBB. A single clean site puts it all front and center where a new customer lands first.
- One tap → the customer calls the shop directly from any phone. A driver searching "auto repair near me" in Henry County won't bounce to a competitor while hunting for your number.
- The contact form captures a lead while you're under a hood. Every missed call right now is a repair a competing shop is booking instead of you.
- Foreign and domestic, real diagnostics, fair pricing — the site says all of that up front, so the people who call already know they're in the right place.

---

## Objection handling

**I already stay busy on word-of-mouth.**
→ Totally — that reputation is exactly the asset here. The site catches the customers who heard your name but Googled before calling, and the ones comparing two shops on their phone. That's the slice you never see walk in.

**I don't have time to deal with a website.**
→ That's the whole point — it's already built. You don't manage it, you don't update it. Maybe a half hour of your time total: glance at the copy, swap a photo if you want, then we publish.

**$1,500 is too much.**
→ Fair question. What's an average repair ticket for you? One or two jobs you'd have otherwise missed covers the whole site — and you own it outright, no monthly fee. After that it just works for you.

**I'm not sure those reviews are even right.**
→ Good instinct — and I'd rather get it exact than guess. Whatever your real numbers are, we'll put your true rating on the site before it goes live. Nothing publishes that you haven't signed off on.
